content: Walking through the narrow, dim alley, I noticed a strange tear in the air—a shimmering slit that pulsed softly, almost inviting. Curiosity overcame caution, and I stepped through. Instantly, cold metal pressed beneath me. I was lying on a surgical table, harsh lights glaring overhead. Next to me, a tray gleamed with instruments—scalpels, clamps—silent and waiting. The air smelled sterile, sharp with a hint of antiseptic. A chair sat empty nearby, as if someone had just stepped away. Questions flooded my mind: How did I get here? Who had brought me? The rip behind me flickered, a fragile thread to the world I knew. I realized this was no ordinary alley, no mere shortcut. It was a crossing between moments, a place where time itself felt fragile. And now, caught between what was and what might be, I was left to wonder—what was waiting on the other side of that door?
